content.problemStatement:
Chef has an array AAA of length NNN.

An index iii is called strong if we can change the gcd of the whole array just by changing the value of AiA_iAi​.

Determine the number of strong indices in the array.


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
First line of each test case contains an integer NNN denoting the size of the array AAA.
Second line contains NNN space separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ - denoting the array AAA.


Output Format
For each test case, output the number of strong indices in the array.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases ≤ 3·10^5
content.inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Then for each test case:
- A line with an integer N, the size of the array.
- A line with N space‑separated integers A1, A2, …, AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the number of strong indices in the given array.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: overall GCD = 5 (>1), therefore all 3 indices are strong.
Test case 2: overall GCD = 1, no index can change the GCD.
Test case 3: overall GCD = 2 (>1), all 4 indices are strong.

solution.approach:
Let G be the GCD of the whole array. For an index i, after removing Ai the GCD of the remaining elements is gi. By changing Ai to any integer x we can obtain a final GCD of gcd(gi, x). The set of possible final GCDs is exactly the set of divisors of gi. Therefore we can change the overall GCD iff there exists a divisor of gi different from G. If G > 1 we can always choose x = 1, making the final GCD equal to 1 ≠ G, regardless of gi. If G = 1, every divisor of any gi is 1, so the overall GCD can never be changed. Consequently:
- If the GCD of the whole array is greater than 1, every index is strong (answer = N).
- Otherwise (G = 1) no index is strong (answer = 0).
The algorithm for each test case: compute G = gcd of all elements in O(N) time, then output N if G > 1 else 0.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, total O(ΣN) ≤ 3·10^5.

code.Python3:
import sys, math

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it))
        g = 0
        for i in range(N):
            val = int(next(it))
            g = val if i == 0 else math.gcd(g, val)
        out_lines.append(str(N if g > 1 else 0))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
